claims.title: helpers.py ACTION_TYPE_PRIORITY ranks refactor(1) higher than reorganize(3)
claims.detail: helpers.py:15 defines ACTION_TYPE_PRIORITY = {"auto_fix": 0, "refactor": 1, "manual_fix": 2, "reorganize": 3}. This dict is the sole sort key for clusters in ranking.py:209-216 (_natural_sort_key). With lower = higher priority, this means refactor clusters sort before reorganize clusters in the work queue.

claims.title: base/registry.py _ACTION_PRIORITY ranks reorganize(1) higher than refactor(2)
claims.detail: base/registry.py:455 defines _ACTION_PRIORITY = {"auto_fix": 0, "reorganize": 1, "refactor": 2, "manual_fix": 3}. This is a private dict used exclusively by dimension_action_type() to determine the label shown for a dimension's action type in UI display. It contradicts helpers.py on both refactor vs reorganize priority and manual_fix position (2 vs 3).

claims.title: core/registry.py also has a conflicting _ACTION_PRIORITY (submission missed this third table)
claims.detail: core/registry.py:360 defines _ACTION_PRIORITY = {"auto_fix": 0, "reorganize": 1, "refactor": 2, "manual_fix": 3}, identical to base/registry.py. The submission only mentioned two tables; a third exists. core/registry.py also diverges from base/registry.py on _ACTION_LABELS (core uses 'fix' for auto_fix, base uses 'autofix'). No single source of truth for action-type priority anywhere in the codebase.

fix_description: Expose ACTION_TYPE_PRIORITY from base/registry.py as a public name (using the two-registry canonical ordering: reorganize=1, refactor=2, manual_fix=3). Have helpers.py import it from base.registry instead of defining its own copy. Have core/registry.py import the same canonical dict instead of redeclaring it.
notes: The claim is factually accurate and precisely located. Two contradictory orderings exist: helpers.py puts refactor before reorganize; both registry files put reorganize before refactor. Additionally manual_fix priority differs (2 vs 3). The submission missed the third table in core/registry.py. Real-world impact is limited to work queue cluster sort order vs dimension action label — both are UI-only concerns with no effect on scoring. Nevertheless, the no-single-source-of-truth pattern is poor engineering and any future priority change risks being applied to only one or two of the three tables.
